commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the MXC availability/fallback policy and require current-head runtime proof; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this review.; Cleared: No discrete new security bug was found in the diff; the sandbox-boundary changes still need maintainer policy review because they intentionally alter containment and fallback behavior. (sha=c494f80f1d9a13bc9dcd919ceeb48c9d9207b7db)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates MXC SDK/runtime policy, Windows build gating, sandbox shell/fallback behavior, diagnostics, Sandbox UI status text, and related tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only allows MXC on build 26300.8289+, while the PR head explicitly adds build 26200.8728+ and related SDK/runtime-policy behavior. I did not run Windows MXC locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the MXC availability/fallback policy and require current-head runtime proof; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this review.

target.title: Validate MXC SDK 0.7.0 on Windows build 26200.8728
target.body:
Tracking issue: #784
Depends on: #785
Followed by: #787

Contributor-fork note: this branch is stacked on the PR1 commit, but the PR targets `main` because the PR1 base branch exists in the contributor fork, not in the upstream repository. After PR1 is merged or maintainers provide a preferred upstream base branch, this PR can be rebased/retargeted so the review diff only contains the MXC changes.

## Summary

This PR updates the Windows MXC dependency and availability gate for explicit build/UBR validation:

- Update `@microsoft/mxc-sdk` from `0.6.1` to `0.7.0`.
- Replace the single hardcoded supported Windows build with an explicit temporary processcontainer support table.
- Add processcontainer support for Windows build `26200.8728+`.
- Preserve processcontainer support for Windows build `26300.8289+`.
- Keep `isolation_session` separately gated to `26300.8553+`, matching SDK `0.7.0` documentation/source.
- Emit MXC config schema `0.7.0-alpha` for SDK `0.7.0`.
- Keep AppContainer-DACL fallback compatible by avoiding protected PATH-directory grants, omitting nonexistent denied-path roots, and granting only backend-safe PATH directories.
- Bootstrap `TEMP`, `TMP`, `TMPDIR`, and `PATH` inside the launched shell because non-empty MXC `process.env` is not usable on this backend.
- Preserve the compatibility host-fallback default when MXC is unavailable or disappears at runtime and strict fallback blocking is off.
- Add strict fail-closed behavior behind explicit `SystemRunBlockHostFallbackWhenMxcUnavailable=true`.
- Reject custom `system.run` environment variables before fallback while sandboxing is enabled, so unsupported custom-env requests cannot bypass MXC policy through compatibility fallback.
- Fail closed for explicit PowerShell-family shells when MXC is available but the current MXC UI-deny policy cannot support that shell, instead of falling back to uncontained host execution.
- Keep MXC omitted-shell execution on `cmd` for this processcontainer/AppContainer-DACL tier; normal uncontained host fallback still resolves through the host runner.
- Resolve explicit `shell="pwsh"` to an absolute `pwsh.exe` path before launching with an empty MXC process environment.
- Update Sandbox page status/InfoBar text so unavailable MXC clearly distinguishes blocked mode, explicit host fallback, and sandbox-off host execution.
- Add/extend unit and integration coverage for SDK `0.7.0`, build/UBR gates, config generation, fallback compatibility, settings migration, diagnostics, shell resolution, and MXC RW/RO filesystem access.

## Why

The local validation machine is Windows Release Preview 25H2 build `26200.8728`. The MXC SDK probe reports usable processcontainer/AppContainer-DACL support locally, but OpenClaw's current availability gate rejects the machine because it only allows build `26300`.

This PR keeps the gate explicit and narrow. It does not add a broad `>= 26200` rule and does not enable `isolation_session` on `26200`.

## Validation

Final-head validation for commit `f436f087cbf029a67c312ed0a6484fb383bf8061` on Windows build `26200.8728`:

- `./build.ps1`: passed; Shared, Cli, WinNodeCli, SetupEngine, and WinUI built successfully.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore --verbosity minimal`: `2326` passed, `30` skipped, `0` failed.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore --verbosity minimal`: `1091` passed, `0` failed.
- Focused PowerShell fallback regression tests: `4` passed, `0` failed.
- Real MXC focused proof with `OPENCLAW_RUN_INTEGRATION=1` and `OPENCLAW_WXC_EXEC=<SDK x64 wxc-exec.exe>`: `89` passed, `0` failed, with no MXC skip markers.
- Real MXC diagnostics included `executor=mxc-direct-appc`, `contained=True`, config `version=0.7.0-alpha`, `envKeys=[]`, `network={defaultPolicy=block,enforcementMode=capabilities}`, `ui={disable=True,clipboard=none,injection=False}`, and `containment=mxc`.
- `SystemRun_FilesystemAccessMatrix_EnforcesReadwriteAndReadonlyPaths` passed, proving RW grants can write/read and RO grants can read but not write.
- Direct `wxc-exec --probe` reported `tier=appcontainer-dacl` on Windows build `26200.8728`.
- Clean app launch proof using `run-app-local.ps1 -NoBuild -AllowNonMain -DataDir <fresh temp dir>` launched `OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.exe`, kept the process alive, created an isolated `openclaw-tray.log`, logged `Application started (WinUI 3)`, and the test process/WSL keepalive were cleaned up.
- `openclaw-autoreview --mode branch --base fork/feature/locale-safe-windows-tests --engine codex --model gpt-5.5 --thinking high`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings; overall `patch is correct (0.78)`.

## Behavior Contract

This PR intentionally preserves the current compatibility posture while making strict behavior explicit:

- MXC available: use contained processcontainer/AppContainer-DACL execution.
- MXC unavailable with default settings: preserve compatibility host fallback.
- MXC unavailable with `SystemRunBlockHostFallbackWhenMxcUnavailable=true`: fail closed.
- Sandbox disabled: explicit host execution.
- Unsupported explicit PowerShell-family shell while MXC is otherwise available: fail closed, because host fallback would cross the sandbox boundary for a shell-selection problem rather than an MXC-availability problem.

Custom `system.run` environment variables remain rejected before fallback while sandboxing is enabled, so unsupported custom-env requests cannot bypass MXC policy through compatibility fallback.

## Validation boundaries

- PR2 now claims direct real-MXC backend proof plus clean app-start proof for the SDK `0.7.0` / build `26200.8728` support-policy slice.
- Full Gateway-mode Companion pairing/runtime proof remains in PR3.
- Installed Companion replacement/profile migration proof is not claimed by PR2.
- Stable-channel Windows `26100` proof is not claimed by PR2.
- The explicit build/UBR table remains a temporary validation strategy; maintainers may still prefer the separate probe-based MXC availability direction.
target.author: theangrypit

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
